A person with XY chromosomes, and therefore a biological male according to both world boxing bodies, has won the women’s boxing gold medal in the Olympics.

Algerian boxer Imane Khelif soundly defeated China’s Yang Liu to take the gold medal at the Paris games on Friday, prompting an instant backlash.

Imane Khelif after receiving her Women's Welterweight Olympic Gold medal ??#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/z8j0QtJsEN

RETWEET!  The IOC has failed women.A male boxer is now the female Olympic champion.Imane Khelif beats Yang Liu.Male power smashes female talent#Paris2024#ImaneKhelif#xxpic.twitter.com/mtgRBZ1kl8

Shame on the IOC for letting this happen.pic.twitter.com/yl1AZPzTHs

Khelif has won every single round of four matches throughout the games.

Nothing mattershttps://t.co/STaNWaCfFW

The boxer was clearly bigger and more powerful than every woman in the games.

A man beat up women and was given a medal for it… think about how F’d that is!https://t.co/npvdIGcpuH

Ask yourself what this is teaching the next generation.It's teaching boys it's okay (actually, it's encouraged & rewarded) to beat up on women.It's teaching girls to ignore their instincts, take the abuse with a smile on their face, & don't dare question it…or else.https://t.co/ZXKtfJEOcO

It came as a surprise to no one.

Wow. I’m so shocked that someone with XY chromosomes beat a biological female.https://t.co/KLAc8QCTnh

Some were happy that a male beat all the women and took the medal, because it will ‘rile up conservatives’ and people who just know what a woman is.

Conservatives fuming right now lmfaooohttps://t.co/zyzGaZRrse

Sooo good ?It’ll rile up the Andrew Tate and Piers Morgan side of Twitterhttps://t.co/iVeBfb7GvL

As we previously highlighted, the International Boxing Association (IBA) held a press conference earlier in the week in Paris and categorically confirmed that Khelif and Taiwan’s Lin Yu-Ting, who will undoubtedly win a gold medal today, are “male” by definition of medical science because they failed previous gender chromosome tests that determine who is allowed to compete in the women’s category.

Dr. Ioannis Filippatos, the former Chair of the IBA Medical Committee, was adamant that the fighters should not be competing as women, stating “Medicine is knowledge, it is not opinion,” and adding “The nature and the biological world do not change.”

In addition, Istvan Kovacs, the European vice president of the World Boxing Organization (WBO), has also publicly asserted that Khelif is biologically male.

“The saddest thing in the story is that the problem was not with the level of Khelif’s testosterone, because it can be adjusted nowadays, but with the result of the gender test, which clearly revealed that the Algerian boxer is biologically male,” he said.

The International Olympic Committee has defended its decision to allow both boxers to compete throughout the games, with the president claiming on Friday that no solid scientific system exists on how to identify men and women.

NEW – IOC president says there is no solid scientific system on how to identify men and women.pic.twitter.com/VdXaXDPtIp

title: ‘Biological Male’ Boxer Imane Khelif ‘wins’ Olympic Gold Medal - modernity
author: Steve Watson
description: IOC claims no solid scientific system exists on how to identify men and women
